duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SurplusGuildMinter.getRewards() checks slashed flag against uninitialized memory variable (default zero) before loading actual userStake from storage, causing all stakers on any term with historical gauge loss to be incorrectly slashed
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the identical code defect: slashed flag evaluated against default-zero userStake.lastGaugeLoss before storage load in SurplusGuildMinter.getRewards(). ECON-H001 selected as representative for its CRITICAL rating, comprehensive attack economics, and detailed victim impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LendingTermOffboarding canOffboard flag persists across re-onboarding, enabling permissionless re-offboarding without new poll, double-incrementing nOffboardingsInProgress counter and permanently pausing PSM redemptions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All threats describe the same root cause: canOffboard[term] not reset on re-onboarding, leading to poll-less re-offboarding and permanent nOffboardingsInProgress counter corruption. OPS-H002 and OPS-H003 split aspects of the same bug. Note: TECH2-H005 has a duplicate ID with a distinct finding about auction finalization (retained separately). ECON-H002 selected for CRITICAL rating and comprehensive coverage of both the bypass and PSM impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GuildVetoGovernor.createVeto() generates deterministic proposalId from timelockId; OZ Governor prevents duplicate proposals, so a failed veto permanently blocks future veto attempts on the same timelock action
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the identical vulnerability: deterministic proposalId generation in createVeto combined with OZ Governor's duplicate-prevention mechanism. ECON-H004 selected for HIGH rating with detailed attack economics showing protocol TVL at risk.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LendingTerm._partialRepay compares stored nominal borrowAmount against dynamically-scaled minBorrow() (inversely proportional to creditMultiplier), blocking partial repayments after creditMultiplier decreases
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same unit mismatch between stored borrowAmount and scaled minBorrow() in _partialRepay. TECH-H004 provides more precise technical detail about the scaling mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ERC20Gauges._decrementWeightUntilFree has loop counter incremented only inside if(userGaugeWeight != 0) block, causing infinite loop when user has zero-weight gauge in set (addable via incrementGauge(gauge, 0))
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 10 threats describe the identical loop-control bug: unchecked{++i} inside conditional block in _decrementWeightUntilFree. Note: TECH2-H003 and TECH2-H006 each have duplicate IDs with distinct findings (loanId collision and profit locked respectively); those other instances are handled in their own groups. TECH-H005 selected for clear proof_of_signal with exact line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ProfitManager.notifyPnL underflows when computing creditMultiplier update if loss exceeds creditTotalSupply after surplus buffer depletion, permanently bricking auction settlement and locking collateral
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the same arithmetic underflow: (creditTotalSupply - loss) in notifyPnL when amplified principal exceeds total supply. TECH-H006 selected for CRITICAL rating with precise technical analysis of the amplification mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GuildToken._decrementGaugeWeight requires issuance <= debtCeilingAfterDecrement, blocking gauge weight removal, token transfers, and critically applyGaugeLoss (slashing) when a term is fully utilized
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the same debt ceiling check creating a circular dependency that prevents slashing and locks GUILD tokens when term utilization is high. OPS-H006 selected for CRITICAL rating and detailed analysis of the slashing mechanism failure.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ProfitManager.notifyPnL skips gaugeProfitIndex update when gauge weight is zero, but amountForGuild CREDIT is already deducted from total, permanently locking those tokens in ProfitManager
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the same conditional skip of profit index update when gaugeWeight == 0, with CREDIT tokens remaining stuck. Note: TECH2-H006 here refers to the 'Profit Locked' instance (not the 'greedy freeing loop' instance in DUP-005). TECH-H008 selected for clear proof_of_signal with exact code pattern.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ERC20RebaseDistributor.distribute() resets the 30-day interpolation period for ALL pending rewards on every call, enabling griefing via small distributions that perpetually delay reward materialization
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 6 threats describe the identical interpolation-reset mechanism in distribute() enabling reward delay griefing. Note: TECH2-H007 here refers to the 'Perpetual Delay of Rebase Rewards' instance. ECON-H013 selected for HIGH rating with detailed attack economics showing asymmetric cost/impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ERC20RebaseDistributor.updateTotalRebasingShares can overflow uint224 when rebasing shares decrease dramatically, trapping remaining rebasing users via safeCastTo224 revert
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 6 threats describe the same overflow scenario: targetNewSharePrice calculation divides by near-zero percentChange when most rebasing shares exit, exceeding uint224 max. TECH-H011 selected for HIGH rating with detailed mathematical analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SimplePSM decimalCorrection calculation underflows for peg tokens with decimals > 18, bricking PSM deployment or mint/redeem operations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same underflow in 10**(18-decimals) when decimals > 18. Note: TECH2-H007 here refers to the 'PSM decimalCorrection' instance. TECH-H012 selected for HIGH rating and more detailed analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Deterministic loanId generation via keccak256(borrower, term, timestamp) prevents multiple borrows per second and permanently consumes IDs after loan closure
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 describe the same loanId collision issue. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the 'LoanId collision' instance (not the 'Infinite Loop' instance in DUP-005). TECH-H013 selected for most comprehensive analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AuctionHouse.bid() reverts when creditAsked becomes 0 at exact auction end boundary, forcing total-loss forgive() path even when collateral has value
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 describe the same discontinuous boundary condition where creditAsked drops to 0 and bid() reverts. ECON2-H012 selected for HIGH rating with detailed analysis of collateral value destruction and economic impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ProfitManager.setProfitSharingConfig stores splits as uint32 after dividing by 1e9, causing truncation that systematically misdirects the residual creditSplit
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same uint32 truncation in profit sharing config storage. ECON-H007 selected for more detailed economic analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LendingTerm.initialize() is permissionless with only assert-based one-time guard, allowing attacker to front-run initialization of uninitialized clones with malicious references
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same permissionless initialization vulnerability in LendingTerm clones. OPS-H013 selected for CRITICAL rating and more detailed analysis of attack vectors.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LendingTerm._borrow mints CREDIT before pulling collateral via safeTransferFrom, creating a reentrancy window exploitable by callback-capable collateral tokens (ERC777/malicious ERC20)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 describe the same CEI violation: mint before collateral pull in _borrow. Note: TECH2-H004 here refers to the 'Borrow reentrancy window' instance (not the 'Deterministic Veto' instance in DUP-003). OPS-H017 selected for most comprehensive analysis of the reentrancy surface across multiple functions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LendingTerm.debtCeiling() divides by totalWeight which becomes 0 when all gauges of a type are deprecated, causing division by zero that locks GUILD voters from unvoting
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same division by zero when totalTypeWeight reaches 0 after last gauge of a type is deprecated. ECON2-H003 selected for detailed attack economics.
